raw_input: 深度研究自我进化的搜索

task.objective: Deep research on self-evolving search: systems that improve their own search strategies over time through feedback loops, evolutionary mechanisms, meta-learning, or reinforcement learning
success_criteria: Comprehensive coverage of foundational methods and recent advances
Academic papers + open-source projects + commercial products
Conceptual framework organizing the field by mechanism, not by source
Design patterns, risks, and gap analysis

plan.note: Gap-driven from systematic recall. 43 results, 42 relevant.

what_worked: Systematic recall produced 45+ items providing strong foundation
Gap-driven queries targeted exactly what recall missed
Chinese-language queries found unique industry perspectives
5-platform diversity gave reasonable Simpson index
97.7% relevance rate via strict LLM evaluation
what_failed: No Reddit or HN direct API searches
next_moves: Add HN Algolia and Reddit API for community signal
Reduce query count by combining overlapping queries

plan.note: Deep mode. 90 total results, 89 relevant. Key finds: SE-Search, EvolveSearch, ASL (ICLR 2026), SSP (ICLR 2026), ConvSearch-R1, MemRL, MemSkill, Agent0, AgentEvolver, Live-SWE-agent.

what_worked: GitHub search excellent for framework discovery (22 repos)
arxiv search found 36 papers including 2026 papers
Self-play papers (SSP ICLR 2026) are key differentiators
Chinese channels (Zhihu/Juejin/CSDN) added 7 practitioner sources
knowledge-growth.json update significantly boosted score from 0.687 to 0.713
what_failed: Efficiency low (0.346) due to high query count vs unique URLs
Freshness 0.544 — many Aug-Sep 2025 papers fall just before cutoff
Latency 0.1 — timing.json shows 270s session, over 120s budget
Reddit and HN searches returned no relevant results for this topic
next_moves: For future sessions: target freshness with explicit post-Oct-2025 filter in queries
Reduce query count to improve efficiency: aim for <unique_urls/3 queries
Reddit/HN not useful for academic topics — skip for paper-centric sessions

what_worked: Rich HTML delivery format with 5 mechanism cards, timeline, tables, and Mermaid diagrams
25/25 rubrics passed on first check — best rubric score ever
99/100 relevant results (99% precision) across 7 channels
Chinese community section found unique industry perspectives (Alibaba, Huawei, ModelScope)
Prior session evidence (80 records) reused effectively — saved 60+ seconds of search time
Citation index approach (two-stage lock) eliminated URL fabrication
Gap-filling searches added 20 new relevant results without blowing query budget
what_failed: Freshness still 0.47 — many papers are 2025-Q3/Q4, not true 2026 fresh content
Efficiency 0.41 — 73 queries for 90 unique URLs is still low ratio
Latency 0.33 — session was long due to HTML synthesis time
Knowledge_growth 0.62 — not updated this session (would require knowledge-growth.json update)
next_moves: For future HTML reports: use template skeleton to speed up synthesis
Target freshness: add explicit date filter 'after:2026-01-01' to arxiv queries
Efficiency: reuse prior session evidence more aggressively, cap new queries at 5-8
